findings.message: SKILL.md references `AGENTS.md` as the full compiled guide but no AGENTS.md file exists anywhere in the repository
'Comprehensive' in the skill subtitle is a vague quantifier that makes no measurable commitment; prefer 'covering 70 rules across 8 priority tiers' which the document already supports
NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 is read from user settings.json and injected into process.env; disables all TLS certificate verification creating MITM exposure; code warns but still applies the value

execFileSync passes a dynamically constructed template-literal script to node --eval; current content is derived from static file paths but the pattern makes future injection easier if input sources change
sql.js dependency declared as ^1.12.0; patch or minor update could introduce a regression or supply-chain compromise

spawn() called with shell:true on Windows when MCP server command is npx/npm/pnpm/yarn/.cmd/.bat; command originates from user-supplied MCP config, enabling shell injection via malicious server config
Same spawn+shell:true pattern as stdio-tools-getter.js; verification path for STDIO MCP servers on Windows inherits the same shell injection risk

import(resolvedUrl) where resolvedUrl is built from ~/.codemoss/dependencies/ (user-writable directory); a tampered package placed in that directory would execute at load time without integrity verification
metadata.json states '40+ rules' but SKILL.md Quick Reference lists exactly 70 rules; the metadata.json count predates the expanded guide and is stale
MSApp.execUnsafeLocalFunction call in vendored React DOM production build — IE compatibility shim in minified library, not custom code
All devDependencies and production dependencies use ^ or ~ version ranges; any future npm install could pull a compromised minor/patch release
MSApp.execUnsafeLocalFunction call in vendored React DOM production build — IE compatibility shim in minified library, not custom code
